metadata.name: Strong Social Connections
metadata.description: Having strong social relationships and connections is one of the most powerful predictors of longevity. The effect size is comparable to smoking cessation and exceeds many traditional health behaviors.

sources.citation: Holt-Lunstad J, Smith TB, Layton JB. Social Relationships and Mortality Risk: A Meta-analytic Review. PLOS Medicine. 2010;7(7):e1000316.

metadata.mechanismOfAction: Stress buffering, emotional support, health behavior reinforcement, reduced inflammation, enhanced immune function, sense of purpose

applicability.notes: Meta-analysis of 148 studies with 308,849 participants. Effects consistent across age, sex, initial health status, and cause of death.

categories.citation: Social isolation associated with 50% increased mortality risk
Strong social connections reduce mortality by 33%
